channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Israel orders Palestinians to flee Khan Younis, signaling return of troops to southern Gaza city
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — The Israeli army ordered a mass evacuation of Palestinians from much of Khan Younis on Monday, a sign that troops are likely to launch a new ground assault in the Gaza Strip's second largest city.
item.dc:creator: MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH, SAMY MAGDY and JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Beryl makes landfall as Category 4 hurricane on island near Grenada
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — A dangerous and extremely powerful Hurricane Beryl made landfall Monday on the Caribbean island of Carriacou after becoming the earliest storm of Category 4 strength to form in the Atlantic, fueled by record warm waters.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Turkey's president accuses opposition of stoking racism after anti-Syrian rioting erupts
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan accused opposition parties of stoking xenophobia and racism on Monday, a day after residents in a neighborhood in central Turkey set Syrian-owned shops on fire.
item.dc:creator: SUZAN FRASER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli plane refused fuel in Turkey after emergency landing: Report
item.description: Turkish officials have denied the claims, saying fuel was to be provided due to "humanitarian considerations.
item.dc:creator: Devan Markham, NewsNation

item.title: Hungary launches EU presidency with Trump-like call to 'Make Europe Great Again'
item.description: Hungary's nationalist government, led by Prime Minister Viktor Orban, has begun its presidency of the European Union as the country faces skepticism from EU lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South African parties finalize Cabinet positions, solidifying new coalition government
item.description: South African President Cyril Ramaphosa has announced a new Cabinet following a coalition agreement involving his African National Congress and nine other parties.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: French far right works to turn election win into power. Its rivals want to stop it in decisive vote
item.description: PARIS (AP) — With the ultimate outcome still up in the air, France's fiercely anti-immigration National Rally and opponents of the long-taboo far-right party scrambled Monday to capitalize on an indecisive first round of voting in surprise legislative elections.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, SYLVIE CORBET and BARBARA SURK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The UK will hold its first election in almost 5 years. Here's what to know
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The United Kingdom will hold its first national election in almost five years on Thursday, with opinion polls suggesting that Prime Minister Rishi Sunak’s Conservative Party will be punished for failing to deliver on promises made during 14 years in power.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel releases director of Shifa Hospital, who claims he was abused in custody
item.description: Israel released Mohammed Abu Selmia, the director of Gaza's Shifa Hospital, after holding him without charge or trial over allegations the facility was used as a Hamas command center.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 18 confirmed dead after female suicide bombers unleash coordinated attack in Nigeria, authorities say
item.description: Authorities in northern Nigeria say female suicide bombers have targeted a wedding, a funeral and a hospital in coordinated attacks that killed at least 18 people.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: German court convicts a prominent far-right politician for using a Nazi slogan again
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A high-profile politician in the far-right Alternative for Germany party was convicted for the second time Monday of knowingly using a Nazi slogan at a political event.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: McLaughlin-Levrone sends pre-Olympic jolt, runs 50.65 to break world record (again) in 400 hurdles
item.description: EUGENE, Ore. (AP) — For the better part of two years, Sydney McLaughlin-Levrone all but disappeared from the 400-meter hurdles course. Turns out, she wasn't hiding or looking for something else to do. Just getting better at what she does best.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Gaza militants fire rockets into Israel as tank advances intensify in north and south
item.description: The Palestinian Islamic Jihad group fired a barrage of rockets into Israel on Monday as Israeli tanks advanced deeper into Gaza, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ultra-Orthodox protest against order to enlist in Israeli military turns violent in Jerusalem
item.description: Israeli police said protesters threw rocks and attacked the car of an ultra-Orthodox Cabinet minister.
item.dc:creator: OHAD ZWIGENBERG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: North Korea test-launches 2 ballistic missiles, after end of new US-South Korea-Japan drill
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea test-fired two ballistic missile Monday but one of them possibly flew abnormally, South Korea's military said, a day after the North vowed “offensive and overwhelming” responses to a new U.S. military drill with South Korea and Japan.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: In a proud and troubled UK town, voters wonder whether their election choice will make a difference
item.description: A lot of politicians have promised change to voters in Hartlepool, a wind-whipped port town in northeast England.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: French far-right scores convincing first-round election win: What to know
item.description: The far-right National Rally party in France scored a convincing early lead in the first round of the country’s parliamentary elections on Sunday, according to pollsters, bringing controversial leader Marine Le Pen a step closer to forming a government.
item.dc:creator: Nick Robertson, The Hill

item.title: US military in Europe on heightened terror alert
item.description: The command told U.S. personnel in Europe that condition “Charlie,” the second-highest alert level. is in effect.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Bolivian president orchestrated a 'self-coup,' political rival Evo Morales claims
item.description: Former Bolivian President Evo Morales is accusing his political ally-turned-rival President Luis Arce of deceiving the Bolivian people by staging a “self-coup” last week.
item.dc:creator: Megan Janetsky, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Storms in Switzerland and Italy cause flooding and landslides, leaving at least 4 people dead
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Storms in Switzerland and northern Italy caused extensive flooding and landslides, leaving at least four people dead, authorities said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US struggles to deter Houthi threat as crisis spirals
item.description: After half a year of conflict, the U.S. has failed to deter the Houthi rebels from attacking merchant ships in the Red Sea as the Yemeni fighters continue to sink commercial boats and disrupt global trade, posing an increasingly difficult challenge for the far larger American military. Repeated U.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Most of the world prefers Biden but is preparing for Trump
item.description: Former President Trump’s apparent victory in the presidential debate Thursday night has added urgency to efforts underway by world leaders to prepare for a second Trump administration, despite international audiences preferring President Biden.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Beryl strengthens into first hurricane of 2024 Atlantic season. Here’s what you should know
item.description: Hurricane Beryl became a hurricane Saturday, the first of the 2024 Atlantic season, according to discussions from the National Hurricane Center (NHC).
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: From classroom to podium: The journey of US student athletes to the Olympics
item.description: Student athletes heading to the Olympics in Paris this summer face a monumental task training for their sport while completing college courses. Universities will be sending scores of students to the games, representing the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas, The Hill

item.title: Twice-yearly injection fully protects women from HIV, trial finds
item.description: The trial showed the new injection "demonstrated 100% efficacy as a preventive treatment" for HIV among African women.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: U.S. couple among hundreds who died in pilgrimage to Mecca
item.description: A couple from Maryland were among the more than 1,300 people who died during the Hajj pilgrimage in Saudi Arabia, Democratic Senate candidate Angela Alsobrooks announced Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Nick Robertson, WFLA, NBC
